name: Kinshasa (Democratic Republic of the Congo)

colors.symbolism: the wealth and the equatorial sun
purity and the river's wake; foaming water

description: A dark blue (navy) field. In the upper half, a large yellow letter K flanked left and right by yellow five-pointed stars. In the lower half, four undulating white wavy bands extending across the field, representing the Congo River.
history: Kinshasa, formerly Leopoldville, became the capital of the Democratic Republic of the Congo in 1960 and a city-province in 1966. Its provincial flag follows the post-2006 pattern of Congolese provincial flags adopted under the Third Republic, charging a blue field with stars and a province initial above stylised waves of the Congo River, here in white on blue. The two stars and central initial format mirror that of the other Congolese provinces.

features.symbolism: the dark blue waters of the Congo River, on whose southern bank Kinshasa stands

features.role: two five-pointed yellow stars flanking a central yellow letter K
features.symbolism: the dual stars represent the city's status as both the national capital and a province; following the design pattern used on Congolese provincial flags.

features.role: the letter 'K' between the two stars
stylised wavy white lines crossing the lower half of the field
features.symbolism: the Congo River, on whose banks Kinshasa is built; the meeting of the river and the city.
